title: × Myrmecocattleya spiralis
intro: × Myrmecocattleya spiralis, also known as the Myrmecocattleya, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between a Myrmecia orchid and a Cattleya orchid. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of × Myrmecocattleya spiralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the Myrmecocattleya is often used in preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: × Myrmecocattleya spiralis is a hybrid orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Specifically, it is a cross between Myrmecophila tenuifolia and Cattleya warscewiczii. This classification makes it a part of the subtribe Cattleyinae. As a hybrid orchid, its classification is based on its parent species. Myrmecocattleya spiralis is further categorized as a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows in the ground.
